issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
DecentralizedVoting.vote(uint256) (contract.sol#75-90) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp > block.timestamp + 604800 && ! proposal.votingEnded (contract.sol#79)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: solc-version
issues.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.20 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.20 (contract.sol#2)

state_variables._REQUIREMENT_hasVoted: MUST create: mapping(address => bool) public hasVoted - SEPARATE mapping for tracking if voter voted
state_variables._REQUIREMENT_candidateVotes: MUST create: mapping(address => uint256) public candidateVotes - SEPARATE mapping for vote counts per candidate

functions.createProposal: Election function - Implement with proper data structures
functions.vote: Vote casting - MUST: 1) Check auto-tabulate at START, 2) Check hasVoted[msg.sender], 3) Set hasVoted[msg.sender]=true, 4) Increment candidateVotes[candidate] - Use SEPARATE mappings
functions.endVoting: Election function - Implement with proper data structures
functions.getProposalResults: Result calculation - MUST be internal _autoTabulate() called automatically, NOT external manual function
functions._REQUIREMENT_autoTabulate: MUST create internal _autoTabulate() function, check at START of vote() function, NOT as external manual function

stage2_metadata.security_summary:
Contract Type: Custom
Classification Confidence: 90%
Access Control: role_based
Security Features: ReentrancyGuard
Implementation: Custom from scratch
Subtype: election

Reasoning: The contract involves voting on proposals, which aligns with an election system rather than full DAO governance. It lacks elements like voting power, quorum, and execution typical of a Governor contract.

classification.reasoning: The contract involves voting on proposals, which aligns with an election system rather than full DAO governance. It lacks elements like voting power, quorum, and execution typical of a Governor contract.
